scripts.preview:
You're probably watching this from the same place I was a few years ago. Maybe you're sitting on a couch in an apartment you hate, or standing on a piece of land you just bought and have absolutely no idea what to do with next. You've got seventeen tabs open. You've watched a hundred YouTube videos. You've got a notepad full of questions and basically no one in your actual life who gets it.
Like — your friends think you're having some kind of crisis. Your family is politely waiting for this phase to pass. And you're sitting there going, no, this is real, this is the thing, I actually want to build something, I want land, I want a different kind of life — and the silence you get back is deafening.
That's the person I'm talking to right now. The one who is figuring this out almost entirely alone. Piecing it together from comment sections and forums and whoever happens to post a video that week. Hoping somebody — anybody — has already made the mistake you're about to make so you don't have to make it yourself.
That was me. For a long time, that was us. And I see you.
